cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about the new George Washington Presidential $1 coin and compares it to the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The question is asking what can be inferred from the passage. First, let's look at the options: A) There are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. B) The new coin can buy more than the old coin. C) The new coin takes on a white color. D) The Sacagawea Golden Dollar is bigger. The passage says, "The Presidential $1 coins will be the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. However, there is an important difference. For the first time since the 1930s, there is an inscription on the edge of each coin." So, the new Presidential $1 coins are the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The difference is the inscription on the edge. Looking at option A: The passage doesn't mention anything about the Sacagawea Dollar having no words. It just says the new coins have inscriptions on the edge, which the Sacagawea doesn't.
